channel.title: archlinux on Ayham's Online Website
channel.description: Recent content in archlinux on Ayham's Online Website

item.title: Hardening the Archlinux WiFi Network Stack
item.description: A wise Archlinux user once said: One shall secure his network stack, or shall suffer network leaks. - a wise internet enjoyer Due to ArchLinux’s design of customizability and Laissez-Faire-like package and system architecture policies, one has to make the incomprehnsibly (indeed) difficult choice of choosing his network stack. Generally speaking, a decent choice for estabilishing WiFi connection, is using iwd. And dhcpcd for ethernet. In this article, I would be focusing on WiFi and DNS hardening.
